short_description.value: SkySense is a large-scale multi-modal remote sensing foundation model (MM-RSFM) with 2.06 billion parameters, pre-trained on 21.5 million temporal sequences from optical and SAR data. It features a factorized multi-modal spatiotemporal encoder, multi-granularity contrastive learning, and geo-context prototype learning for universal Earth Observation imagery interpretation.

backbone.value: Swin Transformer (Swin-H) for HSROI, ViT-L for TMsI and TSARI

pretext_training_type.value: Multi-Granularity Contrastive Learning, Cross-Modal Alignment, Unsupervised Geo-Context Prototype Learning

pretraining.value: Self-supervised pretraining with multi-granularity contrastive learning at pixel, object, and image levels, cross-modal alignment, and unsupervised geo-context prototype learning using a teacher-student framework.

dataset.value: Multi-modal remote sensing dataset (HSROIs from WorldView-3/4, TMsI from Sentinel-2, TSARI from Sentinel-1)

sampling.value: Randomly select 20 images for TMsI sequence, 10 images for TSARI sequence

processing_level.value: Sentinel-2 Level-2A, Sentinel-1 Level-1 IW, GRD

cloud_cover.value: Images with cloud coverage >1% filtered out (Sentinel-2)
